critical_issues.description: Function usb_open_device() has no return type specified
critical_issues.issue_summary: Missing return type declaration before function name
critical_issues.recommendation: Add appropriate return type (likely usb_device_t *) before function name in function declaration

critical_issues.description: Double cleanup: usb_close_device() and fw_free() called twice
critical_issues.issue_summary: Resource cleanup called multiple times, potentially causing double-free
critical_issues.recommendation: Consolidate cleanup into single exit block after fwloader_run() returns

high_severity_issues.description: No check for usb_open_device() return before using dev
high_severity_issues.issue_summary: Assumes device successfully opened, but NULL could be returned on failure
high_severity_issues.recommendation: Add NULL check after usb_open_device() call before using dev pointer

high_severity_issues.description: No check for dev being NULL after fwloader_run() fails
high_severity_issues.issue_summary: Calls usb_close_device() with potentially NULL dev parameter
high_severity_issues.recommendation: Check dev != NULL before calling usb_close_device()

high_severity_issues.description: If USB open fails, fw_buf not freed if fw_load() succeeded
high_severity_issues.issue_summary: Memory leak if firmware buffer already allocated when USB open fails
high_severity_issues.recommendation: Check fw_buf != NULL before fw_free() call to avoid double-free

high_severity_issues.description: If fwloader_run() fails, cleanup not called on success path
high_severity_issues.issue_summary: Resource cleanup logic inconsistent and potentially incomplete
high_severity_issues.recommendation: Consolidate cleanup into single exit path using goto or early returns

medium_severity_issues.issue_summary: GetModuleFileNameA may write MAX_PATH bytes without ensuring null termination
medium_severity_issues.recommendation: Ensure null termination and handle overflow case properly

medium_severity_issues.description: USB open error not distinguished in error messages
medium_severity_issues.issue_summary: Error codes from USB open not used consistently in error messages
medium_severity_issues.recommendation: Add more detailed error handling and message differentiation

medium_severity_issues.description: Inconsistent argument parsing for firmware path
medium_severity_issues.issue_summary: -f --firmware format causes double increment, inconsistent handling
medium_severity_issues.recommendation: Handle both formats consistently or standardize on single format

medium_severity_issues.description: Incorrect test initialization for error path
medium_severity_issues.issue_summary: Tests memory value instead of NULL, may not detect real NULL return
medium_severity_issues.recommendation: Initialize buf_out to NULL and test for NULL return value

medium_severity_issues.description: Error codes hidden from caller interface
medium_severity_issues.issue_summary: Caller cannot access detailed error codes through function return type
medium_severity_issues.recommendation: Add error_code field to usb_device_t structure or use separate error parameter

medium_severity_issues.description: Missing NULL check for return value
medium_severity_issues.issue_summary: Return value of GetModuleFileNameA not fully validated
medium_severity_issues.recommendation: Add explicit NULL check and handle edge cases

low_severity_issues.description: Missing output file specification for executable
low_severity_issues.issue_summary: Target name doesn't match expected executable name
low_severity_issues.recommendation: Add OUTPUT_NAME samsung_fwloader or rename target

low_severity_issues.issue_summary: Public API not documented through header file
low_severity_issues.recommendation: Create main.h with API documentation or document inline

low_severity_issues.description: Hardcoded auto-detach flag may not be supported on older libusb
low_severity_issues.issue_summary: Assumes modern libusb version with auto-detach functionality
low_severity_issues.recommendation: Add version check or fallback for older libusb versions

low_severity_issues.issue_summary: Target devices hardcoded, not configurable through parameters
low_severity_issues.recommendation: Make VID/PID configurable through command-line arguments

low_severity_issues.description: Missing width specification in printf format
low_severity_issues.issue_summary: No minimum width specifier for device addresses
low_severity_issues.recommendation: Add width specifiers for consistent formatting

low_severity_issues.code_snippet:
if (!dev || !dev->handle || (!fw_buf && fw_size != 0)) {
    fprintf(stderr, "error: invalid fwloader arguments\n");
low_severity_issues.issue_summary: Error message doesn't specify which argument is invalid
low_severity_issues.recommendation: Add more detailed error information about which argument is invalid

low_severity_issues.description: Help text doesn't explain firmware format requirements
low_severity_issues.code_snippet: printf("  -f, --firmware <path>   Path to firmware file (default: FalconFW.bin in same dir)\n");
low_severity_issues.issue_summary: Users may not know expected firmware file format or size
low_severity_issues.recommendation: Add documentation about required firmware format and size

low_severity_issues.description: Race condition with static device allocation
low_severity_issues.issue_summary: Static device allocation may cause issues in concurrent test scenarios
low_severity_issues.recommendation: Use dynamic allocation like real implementation

low_severity_issues.description: Static device state persists across test runs
low_severity_issues.code_snippet:
usb_device_t *usb_open_device(uint16_t vid, uint16_t pid, int *error_out) {
    static usb_device_t dev = { 0 };
low_severity_issues.issue_summary: Static device state persists between test calls
low_severity_issues.recommendation: Reinitialize device state properly between tests

low_severity_issues.issue_summary: Device struct initialized but then overwritten by usb_open_device
No fallback message if all firmware path options fail
low_severity_issues.recommendation: Add fallback message when default path cannot be built

low_severity_issues.description: USB device info not shown in list mode
low_severity_issues.issue_summary: No indication of which specific Samsung models found
low_severity_issues.recommendation: Add more detailed device information in list mode

low_severity_issues.description: Verbose path doesn't show error info
low_severity_issues.issue_summary: No verbose output for error messages
low_severity_issues.recommendation: Add error information to verbose output when operation fails

low_severity_issues.description: Insufficient error message for interface claim failure
low_severity_issues.code_snippet: fprintf(stderr, "error: failed to claim interface 0: %s\n", libusb_error_name(ret));
low_severity_issues.issue_summary: Error message doesn't explain why interface claim might fail
low_severity_issues.recommendation: Add more context about possible cause of claim failure

immediate_actions: Fix compilation error: add return type to usb_open_device()
Fix double-free bug in main.c cleanup logic
Add NULL pointer checks after usb_open_device() call
Consolidate resource cleanup paths in main.c
recommended_improvements: Improve buffer overflow handling in GetModuleFileNameA
Create main.h documentation file for public API
Add proper error code propagation mechanism
Enhance error messages with specific USB error details
future_enhancements: Make VID/PID configurable through command-line arguments
Add configuration file support for defaults
Improve test coverage for edge cases
Add unit tests for USB layer directly
Implement verbose mode for detailed USB operations
Add progress indication for firmware upload
